title: Rapunzel
lines: Once upon a time there lived a man and his wife who had long desired a child.
At last their wish was granted, and a daughter was born to them.
But on the night of her birth, an old witch came and took the child away.
The child was named Rapunzel and was locked away in a high tower in the middle of a forest.
The tower had no doors or stairs, only a single window at the very top.
The witch visited the girl every day and was the only person she ever saw.
Rapunzel was very lonely, but as she grew older, she learned to sing beautifully.
Her hair grew long and golden, and the witch used it to climb up to the window.
One day a prince was riding through the forest and heard Rapunzel's beautiful voice.
He was enchanted and called to her, and Rapunzel, never having seen anyone but the witch, was delighted to have someone new to talk to.
The prince came every evening, and the two fell in love.
He persuaded her to let down her hair so he could climb up to the window.
One evening the witch discovered the prince in the tower.
In her fury, she cut off Rapunzel's beautiful hair.
Then she sent the girl away into the forest to suffer and despair.
The prince, coming the next evening and finding only the witch, was thrown from the tower and blinded by the thorns below.
He wandered in the forest for many years, sad and hopeless.
Meanwhile, Rapunzel had a son and a daughter in the forest.
One day the prince's wandering brought him to the forest where Rapunzel lived.
He heard her voice singing and followed it.
When he found her, Rapunzel wept with joy and hugged him.
Her tears of happiness fell upon his eyes and healed them, and he could see again.
They made their way to the prince's kingdom and were married.
And they lived together in happiness for the rest of their lives.
